name: Lamb with Onions
recipeIngredient: 1 ginger piece, 1 inch long - coarsely chopped
2½ lb lamb shoulder, boneless and trimmed of fat
recipeInstructions:
It's preferable to use freshly ground cumin and coriander seeds yourself,
but in a pinch, store-bought ground cumin and coriander will do.
Halve three onions lengthwise, then cut crosswise into very thin half rings. Chop the fourth onion finely. Keep two types of onions separately.
Put garlic and ginger into a container of blender or food processor; add 3 tbsp of water and process until smooth.
Heat oil in a large, wide saucepan over medium-high heat. When hot, add sliced onions. Stir gently and fry for 20 to 25 minutes, or until onions are reddish-brown. If necessary, reduce heat near the end of cooking time. Remove onions with a slotted spoon; spread on a cookie sheet lined with paper towel.
Add cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves to hot oil in the pan. Stir for 5 seconds over medium-high heat. Add 10 cubes of meat or as many as the pan will hold in a single layer. (Do not crowd the pan.) Brown the meat on one side; turn and brown the meat on the other side. Remove with a slotted spoon; set aside. Repeat with remaining meat.
Add chopped onion to oil. Stir and fry on medium heat until brown around the edges.
Add garlic-ginger paste. Stir and fry until all water in it seems to boil away, about 2 minutes. Reduce heat.
Add coriander and cumin; fry for 30 seconds.
Add 1 tbsp yogurt; stir and fry, incorporating into sauce. Add another tablespoon of yogurt, incorporating it into the sauce. Repeat with remaining yogurt.
Add meat and accumulated juices in a bowl, remaining water, cayenne, and salt. Stir to mix. Bring to simmer. Cover, reduce heat to low and cook for 1 hour or until lamb is tender.
Add fried onions and garam marsala. Stir to mix; cook uncovered for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring gently.
Remove from heat and let the pan sit a couple of minutes. Fat will rise to the top; remove with a spoon.
SERVES: 6
